锘縄diom (Gonja): Bii k蓻 ba艐a n naa a l蓻.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed comparative clause with noun subject and locative-prepositional complement; proverb-like declarative form.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A young or inexperienced person may still understand what elders overlook.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Bii l蓻蓻 k蓻 n ba艐a b蓴蓴 n k蓻 o mi.
Usage Contexts: Family advice; youth鈥揺lder dialogue; community meetings; teaching; respectful disagreement
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to intergenerational listening in Gonja households and chiefdom discussions, where children may notice practical details while elders debate authority.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /b矛藧 k蓻虂 b谩艐脿 艅 n脿藧 脿 l蓻虂/
First Example Sentence: Nii艐wura n ka a y蓻, m bii k蓻 ba艐a n naa a l蓻.
Second Example Sentence: Kum蓴蓴b蓴 no lee w蓴, ama bii k蓻 ba艐a n naa a l蓻.
Third Example Sentence: N na艐 kp蓻蓻 l蓻, bii k蓻 ba艐a n naa a l蓻, y蓻 ka o y蓻蓻.

锘縄diom (Gonja): Kakpala n ny蓻 k蓻 a y蓻 b蓴蓴.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metaphorical declarative clause with animal noun subject and complement clause; fixed proverb frame.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A restless or noisy person is usually reacting to something hidden.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: O y蓻 bi ka o n蓴蓴 n b蓻 k蓻, b蓴蓴 n la o nii艐.
Usage Contexts: Conflict mediation; gossip; family tension; politics; storytelling
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Draws on village observation of fowls and small animals: sudden noise often signals danger, hunger, or disturbance nearby.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /k脿kp谩l脿 艅 刹蓻虂 k蓻虂 脿 y蓻虂 b蓴虂藧/
First Example Sentence: Kakpala n ny蓻 k蓻 a y蓻 b蓴蓴, ka o n蓴蓴 za艐 k蓻 f蓴.
Second Example Sentence: An蓴蓴 l蓻 n s蓴 ka, kakpala n ny蓻 k蓻 a y蓻 b蓴蓴.
Third Example Sentence: Ka baa艐 s蓴 k蓻 o b蓻, kakpala n ny蓻 k蓻 a y蓻 b蓴蓴.

锘縄diom (Gonja): J蓴艐 n tuu n na k蓴蓴.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed metaphorical clause with body-part noun phrase and motion verb; compact declarative idiom.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone has become proud, stubborn, or difficult to advise.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: O ny蓻 kab蓴艐蓴, o b蓻蓻 f蓴 n蓻艐 o y蓻.
Usage Contexts: Workplace; peer criticism; leadership; youth advice; humor
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Uses the neck as a symbol of posture and humility; in many Gonja settings, an upright stiff neck suggests pride before elders.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /d汀蕭蓴虂艐 艅 t霉藧 艅 n脿 k蓴虂藧/
First Example Sentence: Ka o nya sika k蓻, j蓴艐 n tuu n na k蓴蓴.
Second Example Sentence: M b蓻蓻 o l蓻, ama j蓴艐 n tuu n na k蓴蓴.
Third Example Sentence: J蓴艐 n tuu n na k蓴蓴, y蓻 ka o t蓴r蓴 b蓻蓻 f蓴.

锘縄diom (Gonja): N蓴蓴 l蓻 n di a wura.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metonymic declarative clause with mouth/word subject and transitive verb; fixed moral idiom.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: One鈥檚 own words have caused trouble or exposed one鈥檚 intentions.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: O na艐 y蓴蓴 n蓴蓴 ka o y蓻 la f蓴.
Usage Contexts: Advice; court-palace speech; politics; family disputes; cautionary talk
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects the value placed on careful speech before chiefs, elders, and family heads in Gonja communities.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /n蓴虂藧 l蓻虂 艅 d矛 脿 w煤r脿/
First Example Sentence: N蓴蓴 l蓻 n di a wura, nti y蓻 ka f蓴 ny蓻蓻 m蓴艐.
Second Example Sentence: Bi艐 na艐 kpa艐 a艐w蓻, n蓴蓴 l蓻 n di a wura.
Third Example Sentence: O ma艐 y蓻 k蓻 o b蓻 m蓴艐, n蓴蓴 l蓻 n di a wura.

锘縄diom (Gonja): Kpa艐gba艐 l蓻 n b蓴 a s蓴艐.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metaphorical declarative clause with abstract noun subject and verb-object predicate.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Hardship or pressure has revealed the truth about a person or situation.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Kum蓴蓴 n baa ka b蓴蓴 b蓻蓻 f蓴 k蓻.
Usage Contexts: Crisis talk; leadership assessment; community work; farming; moral teaching
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with farming and communal labor, where difficulty shows who is dependable when the work becomes heavy.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /kp谩艐gb脿艐 l蓻虂 艅 b蓴虂 脿 s蓴虂艐/
First Example Sentence: Kpa艐gba艐 l蓻 n b蓴 a s蓴艐, nti y蓻 mi naa n蓻艐 n na y蓻.
Second Example Sentence: Sa艐 no n be, kpa艐gba艐 l蓻 n b蓴 a s蓴艐.
Third Example Sentence: Kpa艐gba艐 l蓻 n b蓴 a s蓴艐 ka ba ny蓻 n蓻艐 k蓻 ba taa.

锘縄diom (Gonja): N sa艐 n y蓻 n蓻艐 a b蓻 k蓴蓴.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Temporal-metaphorical clause with time noun subject and completive predicate; proverb-like fixed clause.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Time will reveal what is hidden; patience will clarify the matter.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ka y蓻 gbi, b蓴蓴 la艐 b蓻蓻 f蓴.
Usage Contexts: Patience advice; dispute resolution; family matters; investigation; planning
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects elder-style counsel in Damongo and surrounding communities: not every matter is solved by immediate argument.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /艅 s谩艐 艅 y蓻虂 n蓻虂艐 脿 b蓻虂 k蓴虂藧/
First Example Sentence: Fo maa艐 gba艐, n sa艐 n y蓻 n蓻艐 a b蓻 k蓴蓴.
Second Example Sentence: N sa艐 n y蓻 n蓻艐 a b蓻 k蓴蓴, y蓻 na艐 ti o n蓴蓴 d蓻.
Third Example Sentence: Ba ka b蓴蓴 daa, ama n sa艐 n y蓻 n蓻艐 a b蓻 k蓴蓴.

锘縄diom (Gonja): A ch蓻w蓴 b蓻 d蓴蓴 f蓴 k蓻 a ch蓻w蓴.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Reflexive-like fixed clause with repeated noun phrase and existential predicate.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A person鈥檚 true character eventually shows itself.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: O m蓴艐 b蓴蓴 n蓻艐 o ny蓻 la y蓴蓴 f蓴.
Usage Contexts: Character judgment; marriage advice; leadership; friendship; cautionary storytelling
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Grounded in social reputation: character is known over repeated encounters, not by one public performance.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /脿 t汀蕛蓻虂w蓴虂 b蓻虂 d蓴虂藧 f蓴虂 k蓻虂 脿 t汀蕛蓻虂w蓴虂/
First Example Sentence: Ka y蓻 b蓻 n蓻艐 o l蓻, a ch蓻w蓴 b蓻 d蓴蓴 f蓴 k蓻 a ch蓻w蓴.
Second Example Sentence: A ch蓻w蓴 b蓻 d蓴蓴 f蓴 k蓻 a ch蓻w蓴, nti y蓻 maa艐 y蓻 ga艐.
Third Example Sentence: O y蓴蓴 艐w蓻蓻, ama a ch蓻w蓴 b蓻 d蓴蓴 f蓴 k蓻 a ch蓻w蓴.

锘縄diom (Gonja): S蓴艐 n蓻艐 b蓴蓴 d蓻, o ba艐a y蓻 m蓴.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Balanced conditional-like proverb with truth noun and cognitive verb; two-clause fixed structure.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: When the truth is near, even the cautious person can understand it.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ka b蓴蓴 y蓻 f蓴, n蓻艐 b蓻 mi a.
Usage Contexts: Advice; testimony; courtly speech; teaching; community debate
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Evokes Gonja norms of testimony, where truth is expected to become visible through consistent accounts.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /s蓴虂艐 n蓻虂艐 b蓴虂藧 d蓻虂, 貌 b谩艐脿 y蓻虂 m蓴虂/
First Example Sentence: S蓴艐 n蓻艐 b蓴蓴 d蓻, o ba艐a y蓻 m蓴, y蓻 maa艐 p蓻r蓻 n蓴蓴.
Second Example Sentence: Ba na艐 kpa艐 n蓴蓴 pii, s蓴艐 n蓻艐 b蓴蓴 d蓻, o ba艐a y蓻 m蓴.
Third Example Sentence: M ma艐 su, s蓴艐 n蓻艐 b蓴蓴 d蓻, o ba艐a y蓻 m蓴.

锘縄diom (Gonja): Yiri n y蓻 l蓻, a n蓴蓴 maa艐 ch蓻.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metaphorical conditional clause with tree noun subject and negative imperative-like consequence.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Do not speak too early when a situation has not fully developed.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Fo maa艐 y蓴蓴 n蓴蓴 k蓻 f蓴 n sa艐 daa.
Usage Contexts: Counsel; public meetings; farming metaphors; youth correction; decision-making
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in agrarian imagery: a tree鈥檚 fruit or shade cannot be judged before it has properly grown.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /j铆r矛 艅 y蓻虂 l蓻虂, 脿 n蓴虂藧 m谩藧艐 t汀蕛蓻虂/
First Example Sentence: Yiri n y蓻 l蓻, a n蓴蓴 maa艐 ch蓻, fo ti y蓻 k蓻 y蓻 ny蓻.
Second Example Sentence: N ka o ma艐 k蓴, yiri n y蓻 l蓻, a n蓴蓴 maa艐 ch蓻.
Third Example Sentence: Ka kp蓻ma b蓻蓻 k蓻, yiri n y蓻 l蓻, a n蓴蓴 maa艐 ch蓻.

锘縄diom (Gonja): K蓴r蓴 l蓻 n p蓻 a nii艐.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metaphorical declarative clause with animal noun subject and locative/body-part complement.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A hidden problem has reached someone directly; danger or responsibility is now personal.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: B蓴蓴 d蓻蓻 l蓻 baa o z蓴.
Usage Contexts: Warning; conflict escalation; politics; family responsibility; dramatic storytelling
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: References bushland experience and oral storytelling, where animals symbolize sudden consequences entering human space.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Gonja): /k蓴虂r蓴虂 l蓻虂 艅 p蓻虂 脿 n矛藧艐/
First Example Sentence: K蓴r蓴 l蓻 n p蓻 a nii艐, nti o y蓻 ma艐 la艐.
Second Example Sentence: Ba ma艐 w蓻r蓻 ka, ama k蓴r蓴 l蓻 n p蓻 a nii艐.
Third Example Sentence: Ka b蓴蓴 n kpa o j蓴艐, k蓴r蓴 l蓻 n p蓻 a nii艐.
